title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts faces worsening drought despite heavy snowfall, with Central and Northeast regions at Level 3 Critical Drought.
State officials urge water conservation as below-average precipitation since 2024 strains water resources across the state.
Upcoming spring rains may help, but sustained precipitation needed to restore groundwater and surface water levels.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's essay highlights women obscuring boyfriends online, questioning traditional relationship dynamics.
Discussion on whether centering male partners is necessary for women's public identity and self-worth.
Chanté's essay went viral, sparking debates and inspiring art, including a song by the band Anahit.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O’Neill is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides children with serious medical conditions a chance to experience fun in a safe environment.
O’Neill's involvement is personal, as his fiancée’s sister, a cancer survivor, was once a camper there.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles have decided not to trade A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots at this time.
Trade discussions may resume closer to June 1 when salary cap considerations could make a deal more feasible.
The Patriots reportedly offered a first- and third-round pick for Brown, which was denied by Philadelphia.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will return for the Celtics against the Wizards after missing a game for injury management.
Tatum is on a minutes restriction following a right Achilles injury, having played 27 minutes in previous games.
Injury updates: Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic remains out with a finger fracture.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's 'War Machine' stars Alan Ritchson as a soldier excelling at Army Ranger training with a mysterious threat.
Disney+ streams 'Zootopia 2', where Nick and Judy tackle a mystery linked to the origins of their city.
HBO Max's 'Rooster' features Steve Carell as an author navigating life on a college campus post-divorce.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots signed former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker to strengthen their offensive line after trading center Garrett Bradbury.
Vera-Tucker, 26, has started 43 games but missed last season due to a triceps injury and has had past injury issues.
Morgan Moses, Vera-Tucker's former teammate, influenced his decision to join the Patriots, citing the team's winning culture.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Manager Alex Cora identified Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as key leaders for the 2026 Red Sox.
Trevor Story played 157 games last season, leading Red Sox batters in home runs, RBI, and runs.
Garrett Crochet, named Opening Day starter, led MLB in strikeouts with 255 in 2025.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC makes its NWSL debut against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
The team's roster includes 14 players new to NWSL, with head coach Filipa Patão formerly of Benfica.
The game will be the first played on the new grass surface at Gillette Stadium after a recent installation.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts debates ending its biannual clock change, with Senate President Karen E. Spilka supporting permanency.
Bill S.2157, filed by State Senator John F. Keenan, proposes year-round Atlantic Standard Time for Massachusetts.
Experts link time changes to increased health risks, and supporters argue it boosts productivity and reduces accidents.
